data.title: Existing home sales unexpectedly edge down in April
data.description:
April U.S. existing home sales: -0.5% M/M to 4.00M vs. 4.130M consensus and 4.02M prior.

Median existing-home sales price increased 1.8% Y/Y to $414.0K. That compares with $403.7K in March, which was a 2.7% Y/Y increase.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Solar stocks plunge after U.S. House passes Trump tax bill; Sunrun sinks 39%
data.description:
Solar company shares including Sunrun (NASDAQ:RUN) and SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) fell sharply in early trading Thursday as a result of the tax and spending bill that narrowly passed the Republican-led House of Representatives, which could end numerous clean energy subsidies.

Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) is the biggest loser on the S&P 500, -15.6%, followed by index components NextEra Energy (NEE) -7.6%, AES Corp. (AES) -5.7%, First Solar (FSLR) -3.7%.

Among other solar energy stocks: Sunrun (NASDAQ:RUN) -38.9%, SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) -23.1%, Array Technologies (ARRY) -13.4%, Maxeon Solar Technologies (MAXN) -10.3%, Shoals Technologies (SHLS) -9.4%, FTC Solar (FTCI) -9.3%, Complete Solaria (SPWR) -7.4%, Canadian Solar (CSIQ) -6.8%, Fluence Energy (FLNC) -4.9%, JinkoSolar (JKS) -4%, Sunnova Energy (NOVA) -2.1%.

Among the changes included in the bill that still needs approval in the U.S. Senate was revised language that would end certain tax credits for solar and wind energy by 2028 instead of a slower phaseout through 2031; in the new proposed timeline, solar or wind projects must begin construction within 60 days of the bill's enactment and finish construction by year-end 2028 to remain eligible for tax credits.

The legislation also would remove the 30% federal tax credit for taxpayers who install solar rooftop systems.

While the industry anticipated the gradual phaseout of solar and wind tax credits, the new version of the bill accelerates the timeline, Raymond James analyst Pavel Molchanov told Reuters.

The bill is "worse than feared" for clean energy, Jeffries analyst said, but "we don't expect this to last into Senate draft."

data.title: JPMorgan's Dimon still sees plenty of economic risk on the horizon - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon said the "economy has been doing well," but he sees plenty of factors that could increase inflation and tamp down growth in months to come, according to a media interview on Thursday.

He points to the U.S. huge fiscal deficits, that the Federal Reserve's monetary tightening path hasn't yet ended, and serious geopolitical issues. "There are inflationary factors out there," Dimon said.

Meanwhile, he thinks the Fed is taking the right approach in navigating the environment. "I think they're doing the right thing to kind of wait and see before they decide," he said in an interview on Bloomberg Television.

"They have to react to reality," Dimon said. "That's not a criticism... They're data dependent. They have to wait and see exactly what happens and then do the appropriate thing."

While the U.S. economy has "effectively been in a soft landing," he warns that "that doesn't tell you what the future is going to be."

The equity indexes are mixed in Thursday trading. The Nasdaq gained 0.2%, the S&P and Dow each slipped ~0.1%.

data.title: U.S. PMI Composite rises more than expected in May.
data.description: The S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI rose to 52.2 in May's flash estimate, exceeding the 49.9 consensus, from 50.2 in April.

data.title: Gorilla Technology updates on share buyback, insider ownership
data.description:
• Gorilla Technology Group (NASDAQ:GRRR) successfully completed the repurchase of over $1.8M worth of its own shares in April and May.
  • This brings GRRR's cumulative share repurchases under the current buy-back programme to $5.4M in the past 12 months, leaving $4.6M remaining under the $10M authorized by the board.
  • Gorilla's management and board now control ~20% of the company.
  • GRRR will release its financial results for Q1, 2025 in the first half of June.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Affirm and KAYAK announce the expansion of exclusive partnership into Canada
data.description:
• Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) and travel search engine KAYAK announced the expansion of their exclusive partnership into Canada. 
  • By selecting Affirm at checkout on ca.KAYAK.com, approved KAYAK travellers in Canada can split the total cost of flights, accommodations, and car rentals/sharing into monthly payments.
  • Consumers go through a quick, real-time eligibility check. If approved, they can choose the customized payment plan that best suits their needs and never pay any late or hidden fees.
  • AFRM shares up 1% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Pagaya launches asset-backed revolving securitization program for point-of-sale financing
data.description:
Pagaya Technologies (NASDAQ:PGY) announced on Thursday the launch of an asset-backed revolving securitization program focused on point-of-sale financing, Pagaya Point of Sale Holdings Trust.

The technology provider for the financial ecosystem said POSH enables lenders to approve more customers at the point of sale.

The product is optimized for loans with shorter durations – typically six months – and credit profiles in the 600+ range.

The inaugural $300M transaction, POSH 2025-1, involves an 18-month revolving period, and is expected to close next week.

data.title: Platinum may emerge as an upside leader among precious metals – Fairlead Strategies
data.description:
Platinum (NYSEARCA:PPLT), (PLTM) may offer a compelling hedge opportunity, potentially emerging as an upside leader among precious metals (GLTR) as gold’s (GLD), (IAU) recent uptrend has paused, said Katie Stockton, founder of Fairlead Strategies.

During a CNBC interview, Stockton said that platinum “appears to be breaking out from a very long term, a multi-year triangle formation.”

This pattern is considered high probability for follow-through once the breakout occurs, suggesting potential for significant upward movement.

The abrdn Physical Platinum Shares ETF (NYSEARCA:PPLT) is up 18.2% year-to-date, and up 8.9% just in the past five days. Similarly, the GraniteShares Platinum Trust ETF (PLTM) is up 19% year-to-date and up 10% from five days ago.

Given ongoing volatility and a cautious view on equities, precious metals like platinum can serve as diversification assets, Stockton said. She indicated that sourcing exposure to other asset classes, particularly those showing promising technical setups, is prudent in the current environment.

Looking at the broader equity market, the founder observed a short-term loss of momentum in the S&P 500 (SP500), leading to a neutral bias. The longer-term outlook remains bearish based on deteriorating monthly indicators, prompting the view that investors feel like they want to “sell into the strength.”

While recommending a cautious approach to broad equity exposure, she advised maintaining long-term core positions in defensive sectors like consumer staples (XLP) and healthcare (XLV).

data.title: Bayer A.G.'s eye disease treatment gets approval in China
data.description:
• Bayer A.G. (OTCPK:BAYZF) said on Thursday that China’s National Medical Products Administration has approved Eylea 8 mg for age-related macular degeneration treatment.
  • The Eylea 8 mg is being jointly developed by Bayer and Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN).
  • The approval is  based on positive results from the late-stage trial at week 48, which demonstrated comparable efficacy and safety of Eylea 8 mg with treatment intervals of 3 or 4 months.
